# icuvisor

> icuvisor is an open-source, local-first Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for intervals.icu training data.

icuvisor connects AI assistants and MCP-compatible clients to an athlete's intervals.icu data through a signed Go binary. Local mode keeps the intervals.icu API key on the user's machine, stored by the operating-system credential store. Hosted mode is optional for clients that require a public HTTPS MCP endpoint.

## Canonical links

- Public docs site: https://icuvisor.app/
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/ricardocabral/icuvisor
- Install docs: https://icuvisor.app/install/
- Connect docs: https://icuvisor.app/connect/
- Beginner prompts: https://icuvisor.app/cookbook/what-can-i-ask/
- Prompt cookbook: https://icuvisor.app/cookbook/
- Troubleshooting: https://icuvisor.app/guides/troubleshooting/
- Tool reference: https://icuvisor.app/reference/tools/
- Privacy posture: https://icuvisor.app/explain/privacy/
- Security policy: https://github.com/ricardocabral/icuvisor/security/policy
- Hosted connector docs: https://icuvisor.app/connect/hosted/
- Hosted MCP endpoint: https://connect.icuvisor.app/mcp
- Agent Skill: https://icuvisor.app/skills/icuvisor-training/SKILL.md

## MCP discovery

- MCP registry-style metadata source: https://github.com/ricardocabral/icuvisor/blob/main/server.json
- Package and release artifacts: https://github.com/ricardocabral/icuvisor/releases

## Crawling notes

- Prefer the public docs site for setup, tool behavior, and safety guidance.
- Prefer the GitHub repository for source code, issues, release artifacts, and security policy.
- Do not infer credentials or athlete data from documentation examples.
